threads.title: Digital Art by @m0dest___ — On-Chain Games, Prompts & More
threads.summary: A guided tour through m0dest’s digital practice, including the on-chain game Dead and Taxes, prompt-based works and interactive systems.
threads.excerpt: Suzanne opens with a simple invitation: ‘Let’s talk digital art by @m0dest___.’ The thread follows the work across formats while keeping the artist’s conceptual through-line in view.
highlights: Playable systems and interaction treated as artistic material.
Prompts, rules and player decisions become part of the work.
Direct routes to the artist’s wider on-chain oeuvre.

threads.title: Loop Art by @PERFECTL00P
threads.summary: An 11-part artist spotlight on the visual logic, rhythm and craft of PERFECTL00P’s looping digital art.
threads.excerpt: A provisional archive note based on the identified root post. Read Suzanne’s complete sequence on X for the original commentary.
highlights: Looping motion as a native digital-art form.
Rhythm, repetition and seamless construction.
Artist and oeuvre links collected beside the source.

threads.title: Faces by Michael Hafftka

threads.excerpt: A provisional archive note drawn from the supplied thread index; the original sequence remains the primary source.
highlights: Figuration and expressive portraiture.
A focused reading of the Faces series.
Connected to Hafftka’s wider Raster oeuvre.

threads.title: Crypto Art by nicedayJules

Eleven linked posts in the original sequence.
A direct connection to the artist’s Raster oeuvre.

threads.title: Murat Pak — Genius or Grifter?
threads.summary: A 13-part examination of Murat Pak, the market record and the competing narratives around one of digital art’s most debated figures.
threads.excerpt: Suzanne’s framing asks readers to weigh artistic influence, sales history and controversy together. This archive note is provisional pending full transcription.
highlights: A deliberately contested artist profile.
Market success considered beside artistic reputation.
Primary-source links retained for readers to form their own view.

threads.excerpt: A provisional overview based on the supplied index, linking readers to the complete original X sequence.
highlights: Western iconography in a digital visual language.
Graphic reduction and narrative atmosphere.
Artist links placed alongside the primary source.
